title: palaquium engleri
intro: Palaquium engleri, also known as the Engler's palaquium, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. It is a type of tree that is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The sap of the Palaquium engleri plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, with herbal preparations often involving the sap being distilled into a medicinal oil or applied topically.
